title: State government history (CM terms)
description: Per-state timeline of Chief Minister terms, with party affiliation and pre-poll alliance, used to render the colour-by-government overlay (TODO/SOCIO-ECONOMIC-EXPANSION.md, RP decision 2026-05-10). One file per state, joined at view time to a date selected on the time slider.

x-changelog.description: Initial schema. CM terms with start/end dates, party_code (matching parties.schema.json eci_code), alliance, and a regime field for President's Rule etc.

sources.description: Provenance per CLAUDE.md §12 / ADR-0002. For hand-authored timelines (Wikipedia + ECI cross-check), each term carries its own per-source citation in the optional `references` field; the file-level `sources` lists the URLs the maintainer consulted to compile the timeline.

state.description: ECI state code (e.g. S22 for Tamil Nadu).

terms.description: Chronological list of CM terms. Each term covers a contiguous period under one CM and one party. A new term begins on a CM change, party change, or regime change (e.g. into/out of President's Rule).

start.description: First date of this term (YYYY-MM-DD). Inclusive.

end.description: Last date of this term (YYYY-MM-DD). Inclusive. Null for the current ongoing term.

regime.description: Kind of government in power. `elected` is the normal case; `presidents_rule` / `governors_rule` capture central interventions; `interim` covers caretaker / acting arrangements between elections.

party_code.description: ECI party code matching parties.schema.json. Null for non-elected regimes.

alliance.description: Pre-poll or post-poll alliance the CM's party belonged to during this term (e.g. "NDA", "UPA", "INDIA bloc", "Third Front"). Null if non-aligned or not applicable.

cm_name.description: Chief Minister's name in office during this term. Null for non-elected regimes (governor administers under those).

notes.description: Optional context (e.g. "resigned over coalition collapse", "died in office, succeeded by deputy").

references.description: Per-term citations. Each is a URL the maintainer used to verify this specific term. Distinct from the file-level `sources` (which lists every URL consulted for the whole timeline).
